ideation: This sequence mimics a scientist carefully observing an alembic.  The animation will focus on the robot's interaction with the unseen alembic, suggesting a careful and precise approach.  The left arm will hold a (simulated) support structure beneath the alembic, while the right arm will gently manipulate a (simulated) control or observation tool.  The sequence will emphasize controlled movements and a sense of focused attention. The robot does not have hands, so the interaction will be implied through arm positions and orientations. In the first frame, both arms start at a neutral position, at the robot's sides. The second frame begins the action.  The left arm will move to support the object. The third frame shows the right arm moving towards the object. The fourth frame shows the right arm gently adjusting the simulated control. The fifth frame shows both arms returning to their neutral positions.

frames.beat: arms hang at sides, ready for experiment
left arm moves to steady the unseen alembic base
right arm reaches out to the apparatus
gently adjusting the controls with focused attention
both arms return to rest, experiment complete
